article_title: Robot Dogs Win the Data Center Security Contract as AI Infrastructure Boom Drives Demand for Autonomous Patrols

content_preview.title: Robot Dogs Win the Data Center Security Contract as AI Infrastructure Boom Drives Demand for Autonomous Patrols
content_preview.summary: Boston Dynamics and Ghost Robotics report surging demand for quadruped robots at data centers, where Spot and Vision 60 units now patrol perimeters, detect thermal anomalies, and slash security costs as the US races to build more than 800 new facilities.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Quadruped robots built by Boston Dynamics and Ghost Robotics are becoming a familiar sight at American data centers, driven by a convergence of massive infrastructure spending and advances in autonomous navigation. Boston Dynamics reports a ["huge, huge uptick in interest from data centers in the last year,"](https://fortune.com/2026/03/17/robot-dog-patrols-data-centers-ai-infrastructure-buildout/) according to Merry Frayne, the company's senior director of product management, a trend that track...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written Analysis piece with good technical detail and economic analysis. Only 2 sources but both are substantial and well-utilized.
editor_notes.source_verification: Fortune confirms Frayne quote, Spot pricing, $700B investment, data center stats, Subhan quote, ROI claims. IEEE Spectrum confirms Vision 60 specs (112 lbs, 4.9 mph, 3hr battery, manipulator arm, water submersion). Both sources are reputable.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All verifiable claims confirmed. Some Spot camera specifications and Novva WIRE details may come from Boston Dynamics press materials not cited as separate sources, but are consistent with known product specs.
editor_notes.originality: No existing robot dog or data center security coverage.
recommendations: spectrum.ieee.org is a premier engineering publication and should be on the allowlist
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong Analysis article with verified sources. Approved.
